theme.name: broadway_boogie

motif.name: boogie_cell
motif.notes: Reshaped from irregular long/short cell to straight 8-to-the-bar ostinato (root-3rd-5th-6th-b7-6th-5th-3rd), all diatonic to F Mixolydian. Swing comes from swing_ratio, not from the rhythm values themselves — keep rhythm straight, let swing do the shuffle.

theme.notes: Inspired by Mondrian's Broadway Boogie Woogie (1942-43) and its grid of primary color blocks on a yellow ground. Key decisions: F Mixolydian means I7 = F-A-C-Eb — dominant-seventh quality on the tonic. This chord never resolves. It is a color field, not a function. The flat-7 (Eb) gives momentum without resolution anxiety. No bVII, no IV, no modulation. The grid IS the composition; only density, register, and tempo change. Correction: II and V as originally notated implied major qualities requiring B-natural and E-natural, neither in F Mixolydian. Diatonic ii (Gm) and v (Cm) are minor -- use lowercase Roman numerals throughout, plus iii° (Adim/half-dim, passing) and vi (Dm). The vi-ii-v-I descending-fourth chain is the primary harmonic vehicle for high-density movements: functional-sounding motion built entirely from diatonic chords, so it drives without ever resolving to a leading tone. bVII (Eb) is reserved for lower-density/color-block movements (Mondrian color-field concept). FIX PASS (post-mortem + Jeeves & Wooster reference): added swing_ratio (0.67, approximating the triplet-swing feel Anne Dudley marks explicitly in her score) -- this piece never declared swing, which is likely the single biggest reason it didn't feel like a boogie-woogie regardless of harmony. Motif reshaped to a straight 8-to-the-bar chord-tone ostinato rather than an irregular rubato-like cell -- previous rhythm didn't read as boogie-idiomatic even swung. VERIFY 'swing_ratio' against schemas.py before running -- field name is inferred from the remap_swing_ratio fix, not confirmed.
